title: nyc-commons — Place
description: The missing cross-domain join object. Synthesis finding: every NYC domain has its own primary key and nothing links them, yet a resident asking 'what's near me?' or 'who represents this block?' needs one addressable thing. Place is that thing — a single location resolved to its identifiers (BBL/BIN), its full geography spine (all the districts it falls in), its address, and its coordinates. Any agency record about a location can carry a Place, and two records that resolve to the same BBL are provably about the same place. This is the data spine of a programmable city.

Place.description: A resolvable NYC location — the join target for cross-agency questions. At minimum a Place needs enough to locate it (a BBL, or an address, or coordinates); the geography spine is derivable from any of those via DCP's geocoder.

name.description: A human label for the place (building name, park name, facility name) where one exists.

keys.description: Other agencies' keys that resolve to this same place (a Parks gispropnum, a school DBN, a 311 service_request location) — the explicit cross-domain links.
